Meaning, by taking a deep dive into myself, I got a lot out of the grandma and granddaughter wisdom they shared, as well as from the other resources they provided. For me, I had an overall wonderful experience. 

As for Tiffany... Ann and Shannah have created a beautiful gift for seekers that is accessible and easy to incorporate into your daily life. Each video provides food for thought and self reflection that inspires. The links to master teachers and journaling prompts provide for the possibility of further introspection and investigation. I loved learning from these two wise women for the last eleven days, and look forward to returning to this course again and again.

Me encantó el taller! Para empezar, es súper personalizado, las chicas están re presentes en el proceso, te brindan un lugar de escucha para compartir tus reflexiones, dudas, resultados, experiencias. Es un espacio que ayuda un montón al desarrollo de reflexiones, abrir la mente y pensar mas allá. 
El recorrido del taller es muy ameno, los módulos son de una duración breve que te permite con sólo unos minutos al día poder realizarlo sin problemas. 
Además, las chicas no sólo se quedan con la teoría, sino que plantean ejercicios para aplicar cada herramienta, que me parece lo más importante y valioso. Todas las ejercitaciones me ayudaron mucho a poner en práctica lo visto, a pasar a la acción. Me resultaron herramientas muy concretas y útiles para ejecutar todo lo aprendido. 
Otra de las cosas que más me gustaron fueron los ejemplos que las chicas ponían a disposición en cada tema que se trataba. Me ayudó mucho con la comprensión, y con la ejecución de los ejercicios, lo sentí una guía re valiosa. Y no solo se quedan en unos pocos ejemplos, sino que profundizan en cada uno, y brindan, en cada módulo, archivos descargables con más ejemplos y demás herramientas útiles para el transcurso del taller. Y también otro aspecto súper valioso para el proceso de aprendizaje fue la relación de todos los conceptos. Durante el taller, las chicas enfatizaban mucho en la relación que se podía trazar entre todos los temas, retomaban y repasaban muchos conceptos, lo que me ayudó mucho a poder fijarlos. Para el recorrido del taller, me pareció super poderoso poder encontrar y trazar relaciones entre los módulos. 
Me encantó!!! Me ayudó a de a poco empezar a conocerme más a mi misma, a poner en práctica acciones que me ayuden a mejorar, a observarme y poner énfasis en lo que puedo y necesito trabajar, a enfocarme en aspectos que para mi antes resultaban invisibles. Las felicito por el taller tan lindo que crearon!

The Book Heartfelt childbirth was really informative and reading the birth stories really helped me get over my fears and know that I could go into my second child's birth without fear and genuinely be excited about it. I personally had quite a turbulent birth experience the first time around and looking back I feel that this could have been a different experience had I had the tools and knowledge that I learned through the Heartfelt childbirth book & program. To read the stories of women who had similar experiences and gone on to have a very positive and different the second experience gave me confidence that I could definitely do the same too!

The second time around I felt in control and calm compared to the first time where I was scared and panicky. I had had an emergency section the first time around and this time I was confident in my decision to aim for a vaginal birth. I trusted my body and enjoyed the journey to the hospital, knowing that every surge was bringing me closer to meeting my next child. Using Kate's breathing exercises really was key in helping me maintain this calm and controlled state. With the second birth I ended up having to go for an emergency section, but this time around I never felt scared or out of control and felt safe knowing that whatever direction the birth took I could maintain this state of calm and control through the Heartfelt childbirth program techniques and preparation.

I would highly recommend this book and program to assist with the birth that is unique to you and your baby!


I would highly recommend the Heartfelt childbirth program. The exercises from the programme enabled me to move with confidence and felt great to be active. Being in the water felt just amazing as it took any pressure off my joints giving me weightless freedom of movement. It especially took the pressure off my back and exercising in the water was so enjoyable, especially in the later months of pregnancy! As with any exercise you feel great afterward so being able to keep up exercising and suitably for pregnancy was really brilliant.

The breathing exercises also I highly recommend. I used them every day throughout pregnancy, they were key during the birth and I will continue to use them afterward. Practicing these breathing techniques and using them daily has been a huge support for me and they were just amazing during the birth to help me remain relaxed and focused.

I loved learning lifesaving skills as they are a huge asset to have. They are so important and it's great that it is part of this course. I would actually love to continue to skill up in this area after learning these skills.

The Heartfelt childbirth program really helped me prepare and had a holistic approach that covered in balance all the right aspects which gave me learning and confidence to look forward to my baby's Birthday and be excited about it.
Feeling physically and metally confident was so important and using breathing techniques really I cannot recommend Kate's Heartfelt childbirth program enough!

Pros: he gives a good example of how to automate the steps if you have to produce several separate spreadsheets as "reports" for your boss from a pivot table. He also gives some guidelines for when you would and would not use VBA macros and discusses other approaches.  There are not a lot of short compact VBA automation courses on Udemy at the time of this comment, so this fills a need for a lot of people. 

Cons: the macro that is demonstrated is not optimized and the videos don't seem to match what the code is doing. At least from what I could see some of the lines in the VBA were different in the video vs what is required to make it work. When I asked for clarification he sent me to a google doc rather than explaining it in the Q&A. The Q&A of Udemy has the ability to display code and even format it as HTML. I felt this course should have been sold as part of a larger 3-part course, but instead, he talks about previous courses and future courses that explain things like data modeling, pivot tables, and power query.  

But with that said, Lenny is a very nice guy and I think he is trying his best to teach this course with limited time to devote towards refining the videos and making them better. 

If you take the course and get stuck, read my comments in the Q&A because I placed my code there so you can compare. 

Overall, it is "average/good" so I gave it 3.5 stars. It could be 4 if the videos were refined to show exactly what each line should look like and the correct order. Also remove references to other courses and place that in the final video only.  Be up front with you students that if they wish to learn modeling or pivot tables or power query then this course will not explain those things, OR you could make a 3-hour course that did explain those things and in that case it would be a 4.5 star.

I do not recommend this course to anyone. This video has no teaching whatsoever. There are no instructions, ideas, or anything useful to take from these videos. And the artwork is unappealing and appears like a very young child made it. The music is constantly looping, and sounds incredibly annoying.

So what did I expect from this course? 
I expected this course to teach the fundamental concepts of drawing in a child friendly manner. And to have artwork geared toward a young girl (like princess, animals, etc). 
Maybe even have them provide ideas for the child to draw and then let them make their own interpretation. This was nothing like that. This course felt more like I was watching a 5 year old draw, with terrible music looping, and no lesson. 

If this course was just about watching someone draw without any instructions or teaching, then the artwork should have been stunning. This video wont even inspire children, but will teach them how to draw unappealing art.You could get the same level of "teaching" and "inspiration" having your child watch a better artist for free on youtube. And give them the opportunity to learn about color theory, and shapes. Your child can draw like this WITHOUT this course, and I honestly feel like this course provides nothing for a child. If teaching them to copy someone elses art is suppose to be "expressing yourself" then they might as well copy someone good.

This course was fun to take, and there was a lot of content that was covered overall. The game that was made in the end is fun to show off and say "I made that." However, there are a few problems with this course that really need to be addressed. 

First of all, the title of this course. I specifically bought this course with the intent of learning how to program games specifically for Virtual Reality purposes. However, there was ABSOLUTELY no mention of virtual reality throughout any of the 27 videos this course has to offer. While what I learned will definitely be of help to me in the future, I am still incredibly disappointed that I was essentially click baited into getting this course, especially when they even had a picture of a person using a VR headset in the thumbnail for this course. 

The other major problem with this course is the amount of handwaving and lack of explanation of new topics the further into the course you get. By the time you get to the 4th week, you'll be using things like Invoke() with no idea what it does or the meaning behind it, simply because you're following along the tutorial. While at the beginning of the course there was plentiful explanations to be had on what new terminology was or how to use the different tools that were being discussed, a lot of the last two weeks of the course felt both rushed and underexplained, with a lot of glossing over of topics that felt important to understand just what was happening in the code.

While these two faults were pretty glaring, they did not prevent me from having a good time learning throughout this course, nor did they undercut any of the work that the team behind the course put into it. The premade assets were well made, the existing scene was pleasant, and the overall finished project feels like a major accomplishment when looking back on when you started.

I would definitely recommend this course if you already have a small amount of C# experience, through CodeAcadamy or the like, and if you want to get into learning how to code a game that IS NOT for VR purposes.

There are fascinating glimpses of lesser understood aspects of the subject in this era - P B Randolph, Hilma af Klimt and Jung's Red Book. 
The summary of Theosophy helped me to evaluate more clearly what I thought was going on with HPB. I would say that HPB was not ever interested in promoting Christianity (she would have known the Eastern Orthodox Church best) and was in fact developing a loosely Buddhist-flavoured, non-colonialist, alternative to it, and was unsympathetic to all of its exceptionalist claims; Fortune's comments on the Christian Mystic Lodge of the TS were therefore most apposite.  It's more than that: I conclude that HPB is not interested in the Abrahamic family of religions in depth, possibly because she opposes all their claims of revelation and authority. I am sceptical of HPB's claims not only to travel in Asia but also to understand alleged Tibetan etc texts; where did she learn those languages? To what standard? From which teachers? The lack of corroboration by matching up with known strands in culture suggests to me that a lot was manufactured out of imagination, so I find it hard to trust the early TS material at all, after reading it extensively. She was sympathetic to broad tenets of Buddhism but I find no evidence she practised Buddhism. The Golden Rule can be found in all faiths but beyond that I see no commonality. The egalitarian claims of the TS are not substantially supported, either by empirical enquiry of its publications, or by the claims made by actual faiths.
Lots to unwrap here.  
Steiner's subdued reaction to Klimt's amazing art is possibly a sign of jealousy in a patriarchal context. She was wise to lock her work away. It needs probing by feminist writers, I suggest.

Pro
	•	Cheap course
	•	Knowledgeable speaker
	•	Light and swift presentation
	•	Wide range of interesting content.
	•	Review of recent research publications
	•	Model comparisons

Con
	•	The structure of the course could be better. Apply for instance the MECE principle. 
	•	There is no practical side to the course like quizzes, coding assignments or readings.
	•	The instructor’s diction can be better: the pronunciation is fast and not always comprehensible like in “unsupervised”, “algorithm”, “leverage”, “agencies”, “perplexity”, use of grammar is flawed, there are non-sensical repetitions and many stopwords like “you know”, “so that’s that”, “right” and “ok”.
	•	The amount of detail is a bit overwhelming, this is not remedied by calling architectures “simple”.
	•	The credibility of the instructor and the course is not properly established. Is Manish Gupta speaking on his own behalf or are Microsoft, Hyderabad IT Institute and Business School vouching for the validity of the content?
	•	The relevance of the subject is only addressed very generally. Political & societal impact is not considered for the various approaches.
	•	Resolution of the video is too low to discern the small and tiny fonts.
	•	The slides are not available. 
	•	The course offers no search functionality

1. Довольно часто возникают вопросы во время просмотра видео, ответы на которые даються в следующем или через 1 урок 
к примеру: 
хоткейсы и способы включения той или инной ф-ции. В начальных видео автор часто манипулировал с таймлайном и чтобы повторить действие приходилось гуглить как это сделать. 
2. Материал подаеться довольно монотонно
( как с диктора ). Можно было бы сделать видео более короткими но уделить в них больше внимания расстановке акцентов во время вещания урока - нужно думать на уровне того как информация воспринимается мозгом.
3. Слишком много времени уделено рассмотрению функций, и углублению в необязательные детали. 
К примеру автор на протяжении ок. 10 минут включает каждый режим наложения и рассказывает что это такое или включает каждый эффект и показывает его настройки, что спокойно можно сделать в самостоятельном порядке. 
Общее представление о курсе оставляет желать лучшего. У автора есть знания по данной теме но следует усерднее поработать над структурой выдачи материала и его восприятию.

This was an impulse buy a while ago to complement my literature, as I like this teacher, and feel it is important to have a holistic knowledge of all aspects of culture (I have also dipped into architecture), as well as historical and political context. Caravaggio fascinated me, and Picasso I already adored, but the other three are still works in progress, for me! Cave paintings are amazing though (The English Patient!) and I love the (Aristotle) idea that art takes us to nature's unrealised ends (subjective I know, and cliche, but Starry Night to me is sheer perfection, and I tend to agree with Plato that art should be more than just a copy of something). As I was once a student of psychology too, I also have a special love for Dali. However, as with any great literature, great art should leave us with much to think about, and wanting more - and this course certainly did that, so thank you!

The mysteries of Unreal's Physics Asset Editor are poorly documented in general, so this is worth checking out if only for the helpful insights into constraint settings and customizing your physics asset. For the series' purposes intended, the results are honestly a bit janky, but it's a solid starting point for getting your characters not looking like they're made out of clay. Overall, this course shows what it sets out to show, though not in a great deal of depth. I'd recommend at least an Intermediate level of familiarity with Unreal and Blender before taking this course, because there's not a lot of hand-holding here. If this is your first introduction to rigging or weight painting or UE's blueprint system, you'll learn just enough to get yourself into trouble. If you have a basic foundation, and this is a topic that interests you, then this course is worth checking out.

【時間がない人に、特にお勧め】

教材をなぞるように作れば、英語話者に限らず誰にでも伝わる内容になるでしょう。限られた時間で、聞き手に情報が伝わり、認識の変化を与える事ができると手応えを感じました。

　講師のノウハウを惜しむ事なく反映しており、各チャプターが短く、知りたい情報に辿り着けます。時間がないので非常助かりました。

　早速、「提案型」のプレゼン資料に着手したところ、サクサクできました。私の課題は、学んだことをこれから実践していくことです。途中で何を話しているか、迷子になりそうです。特にQ＆Aには自信がないので、教材を元に準備してみます。
プレゼンの基本が分かるのはもちろん決算説明会などの業績評価をするときの数字の増加・減少の表現が一通りまとまっていて直ぐに使えます。ありそうでなかったプレゼンの技法です。

以前も別の講座を受けて、良かったのでこちらも受講させていただきました。英語ビギナーの私にはちょうどよいレベル感でした。英語でのプレゼンの機会がありますので、こちらを参考にシミュレーションして本番に臨みたいと思います。ありがとうございました。
英語の勉強を2年前からし始め、どこでどのように使っていくのか？
という部分に対して漠然とした目的で勉強をしていましたが、仕事上将来海外でも今後使うだろうという指標のもとプレゼンがもっとも大切だと気づき受講に至りました。

ここでは日本でのプレゼンと英語でのプレゼンでは表現が違い、難しい内容でも誰でも分かる英語の表現をいかに使うか。という部分がもっとも伝わりやすく円滑に進めることが出来ることがなにより大切だと思いました。

自分自身理解が遅く落とし込むまでに時間は掛かりそうですが演習などを通して自分のものに出来る時間も作られているのですごく助けられました。

ホームページを作る際に、
・そもそもどのような文章や内容を載せればいいのか？
・ホームページ集客で反応がなかった時、反応をアップしたい時に、どの部分を意識して修正していったらいいのか？
この２点が、一番最初にこの動画を拝見して、「これはすごい！」と思った内容になります。
内容がとても具体的なので、とりあえず即実践！と試しにやってみたところ、問い合わせがすでに来るようになってきました！
「まじか・・・ちょっとしたことで・・・」というのが正直な感想です。
改めてタイトルをみたら
「ちょっとしたコツ」だったので、「なるほどな！」と思った次第です。

再度動画を拝見したところ、
「お客様の声を載せる位置」を調整したらさらに反応率が上がるのではないか？
と思い、これから修正をかけていくところです。

This course is quite frustrating to follow. Each section is a pre-recorded video of the process that he narrates over but it is riddled with mistakes and experimenting and there's rarely any moments of explaining why he is doing the procedures he's doing. There's also many moments where his mouse overlaps the video and it confuses you on which mouse cursor you're supposed to follow sometimes as it's tricky to keep track of everything.

Unfortunately you also don't get to see the end result of his work, just a still image. I expected to learn how to create an endlessly flowing river but it doesn't seem to be the case. I'm yet to complete the course but I will do my best to.

My best advice here is to preplan the tutorial and don't talk over pre-recorded footage, just do the process and explain each step you're taking as you do it. It would be much clearer and more helpful.

Los metodos enseñados tienen buena teoría, pero existen varios detalles que no cierra:
1.- Realmente solo enseña 2 métodos: Entrada con institucional y Sistema Zona 0 (ratios), Lo demas no son sistemas, son teoria de lo que implica el trading...
2.- En el sistema institucional, no muestra donde colocar take profit, solo donde entrar pero no donde salir. 
3.- En sistema ratios, deja muchos temas inconclusos desde desde coloca un rectangulo donde tu quieras......, hasta comentar que no debe hacerse en mercado lateral pero lo deja muy subjetivo.... debería de estructura y tecnificar su concepto, que define por mercado lateral y en que temporalidades. 
Además una muestra de un ejemplo con 50 pips o 100, pero realizando un back testing en metatrader con los mismos datos , no fue rentable arrojo número negativos.
4.- Analizando los resultados de su sistema de ratios (zona cero) en el video 22, sus rectangulos de zona cero son de 400 pips con take profits de 1200 pips , eso nunca lo menciona en el sistema ratio.....
En resumen deja muchas cosas inconclusas, lo que ocasiona que no tenga mucho uso práctico.....

Um curso do tipo simulado padrão tem uma classificação mediana (max 4 estrelas) por não haver mérito em ter video aulas do professor explicando o conteúdo e mostrando como fazer o exemplo diretamente na plataforma. Tem por objetivo apenas cumprir seu propósito (Perguntas, RESPOSTAS CORRETAS e EXPLICAÇÕES).


Este simulado contém erros mesmo de copiar e colar, o que traz uma insegurança da confiabilidade das respostas, além de várias perguntas não ter uma explicação. portanto não cumpre seu propósito integralmente. O trabalho inicial foi bom e abrangente, mas precisa ser revisado com urgência para que transmita segurança sobre o conteúdo.

Ex.:
Exame2 - Pergunta 6 - A resposta da pergunta é a própria pergunta, não havendo uma resposta (erro no copy paste).
Exame4 - Pergunta 13 - A explicação diverge da resposta.

Boa apresentação da ferramenta.

PONTOS NEGATIVOS

Falta de atenção:
• Vídeos upados na seção errada
• Alguns pacotes de assets sem áudios ou sprites necessários para a conclusão da tarefa

• Declarações repetidas desnecessariamente
• Ifs desnecessários que poderiam ser enxugados com operadores lógicos ou switch
• Variáveis descartáveis
• Inconsistência na forma de escrever o código

locationManagerDidUpdateToLocation:fromLocation - https://developer.apple.com/library/ios/documentation/CoreLocation/Reference/CLLocationManagerDelegate_Protocol/DeprecationAppendix/AppendixADeprecatedAPI.html#//apple_ref/occ/intfm/CLLocationManagerDelegate/locationManager:didUpdateToLocation:fromLocation:

So, how can this course be useful?
If you are a beginner the course should be more detailed and explained, for example, the methods in delegates, so a book has much more information.
If you are a not a beginner, and in the Infinite Skills site this course has the title - Mastering iOS Development - Location Services Training Video, (see the word mastering?), this course/workshop/ whatever has none valuable.

I’m sorry Infinite Skills, this time you failed…So my advice is: don’t waste your money in this course.
I still don't believe in the use of that deprecated method….

Я уже проходила курсы Натальи. Но так как работаю маркетологом, пришло время переделывать и сайт. Точнее, в процессе курса я поняла, что переделывать сайт (это прям основная мысль) нужно, когда об этом говорят финансовые показатели или изменяется направление организации, а не просто потому что "надо" (кому? зачем?).
Всем по сайту занимались специалисты своего дела: программисты, дизайнеры. Но Наталья - действительно хороший психолог, и помогла понять, как взаимодействовать с этими людьми без ущерба основной цели - что сайт делается для клиента, а не "для красоты".
Добрый день! Курс оказался очень практичным: для одного проекта я проверил сайт по чек-листу, для другого набросал, что нужно сделать. Думаю, что смогу теперь понимать специалистов по разработке сайта лучше, а ну и клиентов конечно.

I'm sorry to have to write this, but this is a terrible presentation. First, and maybe this is petty, this instructor mispronounces almost every major place name from the region. Instead of Mesopotamia, he says "MesopotOmia" and pronounces the Euphrates River as the "you-fraets" and on and on. If you're reliant on him to become knowledgeable about the pronunciation of place names, you're going to get it mostly wrong. 
Secondly, each segment is just a couple minutes long and he consistently repeats the bullet points up to 4 or 5 times. He just repeats, for example, that the Assyrians were in Mesopotamia and spoke Assyrian, five times without change any details. By the 3rd slide, I found myself fast-forwarding to the end so that I didn't have to listen to him repeat himself over and over and over and over.
Thirdly, he delivers what is supposed to be a history of Mesopotamia, yet he does it in such a way as to confuse the listener as to when things happened. He'll tell you the Assyrians came in at 1400 BCE then talk about something else that happened "up to 2400 BCE" and then switch to "after 700 BCE" something else happened. It made very little sense to me because he was jumping around. Maybe it's that English is a tough language to speak, or maybe it's because he's confused the timeline. Either way, I'd have to write down my own timeline in order to make sense of what he says.
